issue.title: W25: intelligence needs an operating file.
issue.theme: From signal capture to controlled work
issue.dek: A test W25 issue showing how iFeed Weekly Signals can move from hand-built pages to a structured data and rendering workflow.
issue.lede: This pilot keeps the Week 23 format but moves the changing weekly material into JSON. The nine records below are test signals for workflow validation: replace them with verified W25 selections before public publication.

workflow.notes: Test dataset only. Use this to verify CMS/render workflow before W25 editorial selection.

signals.title: AI governance needs a controlled evidence trail.
signals.cardFact: Healthcare AI decisions need source, context, version, reviewer, and limitation records before they can become trusted operating infrastructure.
signals.whyItMatters: This matters because AI adoption without reviewable evidence turns intelligence into an undocumented risk surface.
signals.sourceTitle: Replace with verified W25 AI governance source
signals.expandedSummary: This test signal represents the AI-governance lane. In the real issue, replace it with a verified W25 source from a regulator, standards body, health authority, journal, or primary organization. The operating interpretation should explain what changed and what a responsible team should document.

tiles.label: Why it matters
tiles.text: AI can affect decisions before formal review unless the workflow creates traceable evidence.

tiles.label: Owner
tiles.text: AI governance, quality, clinical safety, regulatory, and data teams.

tiles.label: Monday move
tiles.text: Create a one-page AI use record: purpose, data, model/version, reviewer, limitations, and decision boundary.

visual.title: AI use becomes evidence work
visual.footer: If it cannot be reviewed later, it is not controlled intelligence.

signals.claimBoundary: Test signal; not a verified W25 source-backed claim.

signals.title: Regulatory interpretation needs source separation.
signals.cardFact: A useful regulatory signal should separate what the source says from what iFeed interprets for operators.
signals.whyItMatters: This prevents a digest from becoming overclaiming commentary and makes each action point auditable.
signals.sourceTitle: Replace with verified W25 regulatory source
signals.expandedSummary: This test signal represents the regulatory-evidence lane. In real use, paste the exact source URL, source date, source type, and one factual sentence before writing iFeed interpretation.

tiles.label: Why it matters
tiles.text: Regulatory intelligence is only useful if the reader can trace the source.

tiles.label: Owner
tiles.text: Regulatory affairs, quality systems, clinical evidence, and market-access teams.

tiles.label: Monday move
tiles.text: Add source-fact and iFeed-interpretation as separate fields for every selected signal.

visual.title: Source before interpretation
visual.footer: A source-backed signal should survive an audit trail.

signals.claimBoundary: Test signal; not a verified W25 source-backed claim.

signals.title: Weekly signals need CAPA-style discipline.
signals.cardFact: Signal review works better when every candidate has status, rationale, owner, and next check.
signals.whyItMatters: This turns intelligence gathering into a controlled operating process instead of a weekly scramble.
signals.sourceTitle: Replace with verified W25 QMS/quality source
signals.expandedSummary: This test signal represents the quality-system lane. The future CMS should treat signals like quality records: status, rationale, owner, evidence, decision, and closure.

tiles.label: Why it matters
tiles.text: The same signal may be selected, watched, parked, or rejected, but the reason should be retained.

tiles.label: Owner
tiles.text: Editorial lead, quality intelligence owner, and source reviewer.

tiles.label: Monday move
tiles.text: Use statuses: candidate, selected, alternative, watch, separate, drop.

visual.title: Signal review as quality control
visual.footer: A dropped signal can still be useful evidence of editorial judgment.

signals.claimBoundary: Test signal; not a verified W25 source-backed claim.

signals.title: Trial intelligence still depends on source records.
signals.cardFact: Clinical-trial innovation only becomes credible when protocol, source data, deviations, consent, training, and oversight stay inspectable.
signals.whyItMatters: This keeps weekly intelligence grounded in operational evidence rather than abstract innovation language.
signals.sourceTitle: Replace with verified W25 clinical-trials source
signals.expandedSummary: This test signal represents the clinical-trials lane. Real W25 content should be based on a verified trial, regulator, journal, inspection, or ecosystem source.

tiles.label: Why it matters
tiles.text: AI and adaptive methods do not remove GCP evidence responsibilities.

tiles.label: Owner
tiles.text: Clinical operations, QA, sponsor oversight, site management.

tiles.label: Monday move
tiles.text: Map each trial innovation signal to the evidence record it would require.

visual.title: Innovation needs inspectability
visual.footer: If the trial changes, the evidence file must change with it.

signals.claimBoundary: Test signal; not a verified W25 source-backed claim.

signals.title: Access planning starts before the dossier.
signals.cardFact: Evidence planning should connect regulatory, clinical, statistical, HTA, and payer questions before late-stage submission pressure begins.
signals.whyItMatters: Weak early evidence design can turn market access into a late corrective exercise.
signals.sourceTitle: Replace with verified W25 HTA/market-access source
signals.expandedSummary: This test signal represents the market-access lane. A real W25 signal should identify a payer, HTA, EU JCA/JSC, reimbursement, or evidence-planning source.

tiles.label: Why it matters
tiles.text: The evidence that wins approval may not be enough for access.

tiles.label: Owner
tiles.text: Market access, regulatory strategy, clinical development, statistics.

tiles.label: Monday move
tiles.text: Add an HTA question field to every evidence-planning signal.

visual.title: One evidence plan, many decisions
visual.footer: Access is designed upstream, not rescued downstream.

signals.claimBoundary: Test signal; not a verified W25 source-backed claim.

signals.title: AI-device signals need lifecycle evidence.
signals.cardFact: Software and AI-device intelligence should track intended use, validation, monitoring, change control, and post-market feedback.
signals.whyItMatters: A device signal is weak if it only describes launch or clearance without lifecycle obligations.
signals.sourceTitle: Replace with verified W25 medtech source
signals.expandedSummary: This test signal represents the MedTech lane. In real use, connect the event to product lifecycle controls, evidence duties, or post-market intelligence.

tiles.label: Why it matters
tiles.text: MedTech intelligence must show what changes for QMS, PMS, validation, or clinical evidence.

tiles.label: Owner
tiles.text: MedTech regulatory, QMS, clinical evaluation, software validation.

tiles.label: Monday move
tiles.text: For each AI-device item, record intended use and lifecycle-control implication.

visual.title: Device intelligence follows the lifecycle
visual.footer: A launch is not the whole signal; lifecycle control is.

signals.claimBoundary: Test signal; not a verified W25 source-backed claim.

signals.title: Healthcare intelligence is becoming infrastructure.
signals.cardFact: Signals matter most when they reveal an operating capability healthcare teams must build or strengthen.
signals.whyItMatters: This frames iFeed as an operating intelligence system, not a news summary.
signals.sourceTitle: Replace with verified W25 infrastructure source
signals.expandedSummary: This test signal represents the healthcare infrastructure lane. Real sources may include health-system AI, digital health, data infrastructure, payer-provider operations, or evidence platforms.

tiles.label: Why it matters
tiles.text: Operators need to know what capability the signal asks them to build.

tiles.label: Owner
tiles.text: Healthcare operations, digital transformation, governance, quality.

tiles.label: Monday move
tiles.text: Write each signal as an operating capability, not only an event.

visual.title: From news to capability
visual.footer: The useful question is: what does this force us to operate?

signals.claimBoundary: Test signal; not a verified W25 source-backed claim.

signals.title: Agentic AI needs task boundaries.
signals.cardFact: Agentic AI becomes operationally meaningful only when allowed tasks, data access, review steps, and decision ownership are defined.
signals.whyItMatters: Without task boundaries, autonomous workflows can create uncontrolled outputs and unclear accountability.
signals.sourceTitle: Replace with verified W25 biopharma AI source
signals.expandedSummary: This test signal represents the agentic AI / biopharma AI lane. A real selected signal should identify what the AI system is doing, who reviews it, and where decision authority remains human.

tiles.label: Why it matters
tiles.text: Agentic systems move from insight generation into work execution.

tiles.label: Owner
tiles.text: R&D, data science, quality, legal, regulatory strategy.

tiles.label: Monday move
tiles.text: Define allowed tasks, prohibited tasks, review steps, and escalation triggers.

visual.title: Agents need operating limits
visual.footer: Autonomy without boundary is not infrastructure; it is risk.

signals.claimBoundary: Test signal; not a verified W25 source-backed claim.

signals.title: The weekly digest becomes a management system.
signals.cardFact: The strongest iFeed move is converting weekly signal work into a repeatable cockpit with data, status, source ledger, and rendering.
signals.whyItMatters: This makes iFeed easier to operate, easier to audit, and easier to scale.
signals.expandedSummary: This test signal represents the operating-system lane. It proves the product direction: signals are not just content; they are a controlled intelligence workflow that can become a management layer.

tiles.label: Why it matters
tiles.text: A reliable weekly system can become a paid intelligence and execution cockpit.

tiles.label: Owner
tiles.text: iFeed editorial, data, product, and publishing operations.

tiles.label: Monday move
tiles.text: Move W25 from static HTML into a data-driven issue file and renderer.

signals.claimBoundary: Internal test signal for CMS validation.
